indication: RUQ pain, biliary colic presentation. Rule out cholecystitis, choledocholithiasis, hepatic pathology.
technique: Real-time ultrasound of the right upper quadrant.
findings:
LIVER: Normal size, smooth contour. Homogeneous parenchymal echotexture. No focal lesions. No biliary dilatation at the hilum. Intrahepatic ducts not dilated.

GALLBLADDER: Distended. **CONTAINS MULTIPLE ECHOGENIC FOCI WITH POSTERIOR ACOUSTIC SHADOWING — CONSISTENT WITH GALLSTONES.** Largest stone measures ~1.4 cm in maximal dimension. No mobile stones impacted at the neck on real-time scanning. Wall thickness 2.5 mm (within normal limits). No pericholecystic fluid. Sonographic Murphy sign: NEGATIVE (patient pain-free at time of scan).

COMMON BILE DUCT: 5 mm in maximal diameter (within normal limits for her age). No demonstrable stones within the visualized CBD.

PANCREAS: Body and tail incompletely visualized due to overlying bowel gas. Head appears normal. No fluid collections in the visualized portions.

KIDNEYS: Both kidneys normal in size and cortical thickness for age. No hydronephrosis. No stones. Right kidney 10.4 cm; left kidney 10.6 cm. Bilateral mild cortical thinning consistent with known CKD.

AORTA: Normal caliber. No aneurysm.
impression:
1. **CHOLELITHIASIS WITHOUT ACUTE CHOLECYSTITIS.** Multiple gallstones in a distended gallbladder; wall not thickened, no pericholecystic fluid, negative sonographic Murphy sign at time of evaluation.
2. NO BILIARY OBSTRUCTION on this study. CBD 5 mm.
3. No acute pancreatitis findings on visualized portions; full pancreatic evaluation limited by bowel gas — CT or MRCP would be the next test if pancreatitis is clinically suspected.
4. No hydronephrosis or other acute renal findings; chronic cortical thinning consistent with known CKD.
